purpose: Provide simple one-file bootstrap installers for Windows 11, Ubuntu/Linux and macOS plus an in-system `ananta update` command. The goal is easy OSS onboarding without requiring users to manually understand venv, editable installs or Docker before they can run `ananta`.
non_goals: Do not silently install Docker, Podman, Ollama or paid/cloud providers.
Do not overwrite user config, secrets or local repository changes without explicit confirmation.
Do not make bootstrap scripts a second runtime configuration system; they should delegate to `ananta init` and runtime profiles.
Do not require global Python package installation when a local venv is sufficient.
Do not execute arbitrary remote code beyond the explicitly downloaded installer script without showing what is happening.
success_criteria: Windows users can download one PowerShell script and get a working `ananta` command.
Ubuntu/Linux and macOS users can download one Bash script and get a working `ananta` command.
Installers are idempotent: rerunning updates or repairs an existing install instead of corrupting it.
`ananta update` updates the checked-out project, refreshes dependencies and runs a smoke check.
Local-dev remains the default and does not require Docker.
Docs show safe download-and-run and safer download-inspect-run variants.

tasks.title: Windows PowerShell bootstrap installer add
acceptance_criteria: Script supports Windows 11 PowerShell.
Script checks for Python and Git and prints actionable install hints if missing.
Script clones or updates the Ananta repository into a user-visible install directory.
Script creates or reuses a local .venv and runs `pip install -e .`.
Script verifies `ananta --help` and prints next commands.
Script does not require Docker and does not install Docker automatically.

tasks.title: Linux and macOS Bash bootstrap installer add
acceptance_criteria: Script supports Ubuntu/Debian-style Linux and macOS/Darwin.
Script detects OS via uname and prints platform-specific hints for missing Python/Git.
Script clones or updates the Ananta repository into a user-visible install directory.
Script creates or reuses a local .venv and runs `pip install -e .`.
Script verifies `ananta --help` and prints next commands.
Script does not require Docker/Podman and does not install them automatically.

tasks.title: Installer options and idempotency add
acceptance_criteria: Installers support install directory override.
Installers support branch/tag override with safe defaults to main.
Installers detect existing clone and update it safely.
Installers refuse or warn on dirty working tree unless explicit force/update flag is used.
Installers can be rerun without duplicating venvs or breaking existing config.

tasks.title: Post-install init handoff add
acceptance_criteria: Installers offer or print `ananta init` as the next step.
Installers show examples for local-dev Ollama and OpenAI-compatible setup.
Installers do not store API keys themselves.
Installers clearly distinguish install from runtime initialization.

tasks.title: ananta update command add
acceptance_criteria: `ananta update` exists and supports `--help`.
Command detects the Ananta install directory and current git branch/commit.
Command refuses to update dirty working trees unless an explicit flag is used.
Command fetches/pulls the configured branch/tag safely.
Command refreshes Python dependencies in the active or detected venv.
Command runs `ananta --help` or equivalent smoke check after update.

tasks.title: Update rollback and checkpoint guidance add
acceptance_criteria: Update records previous commit before changing refs.
Update prints rollback command or provides `--rollback-to <sha>` if feasible.
Update never deletes config, runtime profiles, secrets or user data.
Tests cover clean update, dirty tree refusal and smoke failure reporting.

tasks.title: Installer update path unify
acceptance_criteria: Rerunning the installer on an existing install either delegates to `ananta update` or follows the same safe update rules.
Installer and `ananta update` agree on install path, branch/tag and venv behavior.
Both paths produce similar success/failure messages.
Docs explain when to rerun installer versus use `ananta update`.

tasks.title: Bootstrap install docs add
acceptance_criteria: Docs show Windows 11 one-command install and safer download-inspect-run variant.
Docs show Ubuntu/Linux one-command install and safer download-inspect-run variant.
Docs show macOS one-command install and safer download-inspect-run variant.
Docs state that local-dev does not require Docker.
Docs include next steps: `ananta init`, `ananta doctor`, `ananta status`.

tasks.title: Bootstrap smoke tests add
acceptance_criteria: Smoke validates installer scripts are present, executable where applicable and contain expected safety checks.
Smoke validates docs reference real script paths.
Update command tests do not require network access by default and use mocked git/subprocess calls.
Release gate can include bootstrap smoke without actually installing from the network.

tasks.title: Quickstart simplify around bootstrap installers
acceptance_criteria: Quickstart offers bootstrap installer path before manual venv instructions.
Manual venv/pip install remains documented for developers.
Docker Compose quickstart is clearly marked as UI/full-stack path, not required for CLI install.
Examples remain short and copy-pasteable.

execution_order_summary: Build one-file installers first: PowerShell for Windows, Bash for Linux/macOS.
Add idempotency and safe existing-install handling before promoting the installer in README.
Add `ananta update` as the normal in-system update path.
Document safe install, update, rollback and local-dev without Docker.
Add smoke tests that validate scripts and command dispatch without network-dependent installs.

dependency_policy.note: Cross-track dependencies are intentional and must be resolved by owning tracks before milestone completion.

summary_notes: This track is about install/update UX, not Ananta runtime architecture.
The installers should make `ananta` available quickly, then hand off to `ananta init` for runtime configuration.
Windows needs a PowerShell installer; Ubuntu/Linux and macOS can share a Bash installer with platform detection.
`ananta update` should be safe, visible and reversible enough for local OSS users.
Cross-track references to unified CLI tasks are explicit prerequisites, not dangling dependencies.

notes: Task status counters, milestone counters and critical-path counters are internally consistent.
All tasks are covered exactly once by milestone task_ids.
Two dependencies intentionally target external track IDs (todo.unified-cli.json:CLI-T01/CLI-T03).
Added execution_stage_summary for milestone-level progress visibility.
Completed BOOT-T01 to BOOT-T10 with installer scripts, ananta update command, docs updates, and smoke/unit coverage.

end_summaries.title: Analysis result
items: Track structure is coherent: 10 tasks, 3 milestones, and clear acceptance criteria per task.
Implementation is complete across installers, update command, docs, and smoke tests.

end_summaries.title: Corrections
items: Refreshed tasks_status_summary and execution_stage_summary timestamps to completion time.
Updated all milestone/task statuses to done and aligned counters to 100% completion.

end_summaries.title: Improvements and extensions
items: Added execution_stage_summary with per-milestone total/done/todo/in_progress/blocked counters.
Extended summary_notes with explicit clarification for external prerequisite dependencies.
Added analysis_checks for repeatable quality review and consistency evidence.
